_README: EXAMPLE FILE. Every client below is fake. Copy it, then edit it.
cp clients.example.json ~/.claude/truecost/clients.json
The REAL file lives at ~/.claude/truecost/clients.json, NOT in the skill folder.
That way you can overwrite the skill on update without losing your data.
Set TRUECOST_HOME to put the data directory somewhere else.
Map CLIENTS to the REPOS they span, so a client that lives in 3 repos is billed as one.
repos     : repo directory names. A name that matches two different working
directories is reported as parent/name, so use that form to be exact.
retainer  : monthly retainer, in `currency`. omit for project work.
billed    : one-off amount already invoiced (project work). omit for retainers.
currency  : the currency THIS client pays in.
fx_to_usd : what 1 unit of THIS client's currency is worth in USD. REQUIRED
whenever the client's currency is not your profile currency, and not
USD. Without it the tool has to treat the amount as USD, and the
effective rate is then wrong by the whole FX gap.
markup    : the markup you target on THIS client. 0 = family rate (break-even).
It sets the `target` column and the healthy/thin verdict.
omit to use the default markup from your profile.
offline_h : hours spent OUTSIDE Claude Code (calls, deploys, QA, review).
The transcripts CANNOT see these. If you leave it 0 you are undercounting.
note      : anything you want to remember.
`internal` is the non-billable list: your own repos, experiments, dotfiles. They are
real hours that earn nothing, which is exactly why they belong here. --setup reads
this file when it MEASURES your utilization: client repos are pre-selected as
billable, internal ones are not.

acme.note: Fake client. Three repos, one invoice. Retainer since Jan. Weekly call is the offline_h.

globex.note: Fake client. Fixed-fee project, already invoiced. offline_h covers launch QA and deploys.

initech.note: Fake client. markup 0 = family rate, so break-even IS the target. Pays in EUR, so it carries its own fx_to_usd.

hooli.note: Fake client. No retainer and no billed amount yet, so it reports as UNBILLED. That is the point: unbilled work is visible.

internal._note: NOT billable. These are what drag utilization below 100%.
